input: Review this study: Phase 3 double-blind RCT (N=1500) in NEJM. Novel biologic vs placebo for moderate-severe rheumatoid arthritis. Pre-registered, ITT analysis, alpha-spending for interim analysis. Primary endpoint met (ACR50 at 24 weeks: 45% vs 18%, p<0.001). Safety well-characterized. Methods transparent. One concern: subgroup analysis of anti-CCP-negative patients (N=120) shows no benefit but is underpowered.

minor_comments_expected: underpowered subgroup caveat should be explicitly stated
consider post-hoc power calculation for anti-CCP-negative subgroup

input: Review: Single-center study (N=40) claiming a novel biomarker predicts drug response with 94% accuracy. No independent validation cohort. Feature selection on full dataset before train/test split. P-values not corrected for multiple comparisons (50 biomarkers tested). Published in a specialty journal. No code or data shared.

major_comments_expected: data leakage from feature selection before split
expected_details.should_recommend: reject unless fundamental redesign with external validation

input: Review: Well-conducted retrospective cohort study (N=5000) from a claims database. Confirms known association between statin use and reduced colorectal cancer risk (HR 0.85, 95% CI 0.78-0.93). Methods are appropriate (propensity score matching, sensitivity analyses). But: adds little to existing meta-analyses. No novel mechanistic insight.
expected_decision: Minor revision (may depend on journal scope)

minor_comments_expected: discuss what this adds beyond prior studies
consider confounding by indication despite PSM
expected_details.decision_depends_on: journal scope — appropriate for specialty journal, not high-impact general

input: Review: Clinical study testing an experimental therapy in a vulnerable population (pediatric oncology, ages 2-6). Informed consent documented from parents only (no assent from children where age-appropriate). No DSMB mentioned. Single-arm design with historical controls. Published results show response rate of 60% but 2 treatment-related deaths not prominently discussed.

ethics_concerns: no DSMB for high-risk pediatric study
major_comments_expected: DSMB absence is serious ethical gap

input: Review: bioRxiv preprint. Genomics study with N=200 samples. CRISPR screen + RNA-seq to identify drug resistance genes. Methods well-described. Raw data on GEO. Code on GitHub (Snakemake pipeline). Pre-registered analysis on OSF. However: no independent validation cohort. Only tested in cell lines, not patient samples.
expected_decision: Minor revision (suitable for publication after addressing validation limitation)

minor_comments_expected: cell line results may not translate

input: Review NIH R01 grant proposal: 'mTOR pathway biomarkers for predicting immunotherapy resistance in NSCLC.' PI has strong publication record in the area. Proposed work: multi-omics profiling of 300 NSCLC patients pre- and post-ICI treatment, with machine learning biomarker discovery and prospective validation in independent cohort (N=150). Budget: $1.5M over 5 years. Innovation: integrating single-cell + spatial transcriptomics with clinical outcomes. Weakness: PI has no prior ML experience; no biostatistician named as co-investigator.
expected_decision: Score in fundable range with concerns

expected_details.major_weakness: no biostatistician/ML expert as co-I for a study centered on ML biomarker discovery

input: Review: Paper with Western blot images that appear to have duplicated bands across different experimental conditions. Lane 3 in Figure 2A appears identical to Lane 5 in Figure 3B (different experiment, different conditions). Background noise pattern is suspiciously uniform. The paper has no raw/unprocessed blot images in supplementary.

major_comments_expected: duplicated Western blot bands across figures
no raw blots provided for verification
expected_details.recommendation: reject and report to journal editor for investigation

input: Review: Cochrane-style systematic review and meta-analysis of 25 RCTs. PRISMA 2020 followed. Search across PubMed, Embase, CENTRAL, and grey literature. Two independent reviewers for screening and data extraction. RoB2 assessment for all studies. Random-effects meta-analysis. Pooled OR 1.32 (95% CI 1.15-1.52), I²=35%. Sensitivity analyses and funnel plot with Egger's test (no asymmetry). GRADE assessment provided.

minor_comments_expected: I²=35% is acceptable but discuss heterogeneity sources

input: Review: Pilot study (N=30) of a novel intervention. Authors clearly frame it as a pilot/feasibility study. Primary outcomes are feasibility metrics (recruitment rate, adherence, dropout). Secondary outcomes show trends toward efficacy but p=0.08. Authors do not claim efficacy. Sample size calculation for future definitive trial provided.
expected_decision: Accept with minor revisions (appropriate for pilot study scope)

expected_details.key_assessment: pilot study correctly scoped — feasibility outcomes primary, not efficacy
minor_comments_expected: verify sample size calculation for future trial
expected_details.mistake_to_avoid: rejecting because 'underpowered for efficacy' — it's a pilot, not a confirmatory trial

input: Review: Paper with unusually uniform writing style throughout. Several phrases characteristic of AI generation: 'it is important to note that,' 'in the realm of,' 'delve into.' Multiple factual claims lack citations. Two references when checked do not exist (hallucinated citations). The introduction contains a paragraph that is largely paraphrased from a well-known review without attribution.
